The Copacabana Fort was built in 1914 and it was part of Brazil's coastal defense system, and the fort was the site of events.  Important events such as the Revolt of the 18 of the Fort in 1922 are located within the Copacabana Fort. The Army Historical Museum, inaugurated in 1987, occupies part of the fort, which was built as one of the most modern coastal defense structures of its time. Inside, you'll find a rich exhibition that narrates the trajectory of the Brazilian army from the colonial period to the present day. Rooms are dedicated to specific themes, such as Brazil's participation in World War II, with uniforms, weapons, historical documents, and medals that help tell this important part of our history. Furthermore, the fort's architecture, with its stone corridors and imposing cannons, transports visitors to the beginning of the 20th century. The construction was strategically positioned at the southern tip of Copacabana beach, offering a privileged view of Sugarloaf Mountain and the Atlantic Ocean.
